# Andrew T. Rodriguez

> Ph.D. Scientist in Aging Biology and Frontier AI Evaluation

## About

Andrew T. Rodriguez is a Ph.D.-trained scientist in the biology of aging, applying rigorous experimental design and scientific judgment to questions where confidently-wrong answers are common. He uses Claude and Claude Code daily for analysis, coding, and literature work. He is interested in building evaluations that probe where frontier models fail in biology, identifying capability gaps in experimental planning, data interpretation, and mechanistic reasoning.

## Education

- Ph.D., Biology of Aging: Buck Institute for Research on Aging & University of Southern California (2025)
- M.S., Biochemistry & Molecular Biology: UC Santa Barbara (2016)
- B.S., Bioengineering: Stanford University (2015)

## Experience

- Ph.D. Research Scientist, Biology of Aging (2020-2025): Buck Institute & USC, Garrison Lab
- Staff Research Associate II, Stem Cell Aging (2019-2020): UCSF, Brack Lab
- Research Associate II, Radiobiology R&D (2017-2019): Varian Medical Systems (now Siemens Healthineers)

## Key Research

- Discovered nematocin (oxytocin/vasopressin homolog) suppresses heat shock response in C. elegans; first known endogenous inter-cellular signaling molecule with this function
- Built RNA-seq differential expression pipeline identifying 42 DE genes
- Demonstrated tissue-specific, age-dependent regulation of autophagy and HSF-1 activation

## Skills

- AI & Computation: Claude & Claude Code (daily), Python, R, MATLAB
- Bioinformatics: RNA-seq (STAR, DESeq2, clusterProfiler), pathway enrichment
- Wet Lab: C. elegans genetics, mammalian cell culture, flow cytometry, CRISPR/Cas9
- Imaging: Confocal microscopy (Zeiss LSM980 Airyscan), 3D segmentation

## Location

San Francisco Bay Area, CA | US Citizen
